name: Hygeia
shortDescription: Hygeia is the Greek goddess of health, cleanliness, and hygiene. She is often depicted with a serpent drinking from a bowl, a symbol of medicine.
description: Hygeia is a Greek goddess, daughter of Asclepius, the god of medicine, and Epione. She embodies health, cleanliness, and hygiene, playing a crucial role in the Asclepiadae's healing practices. Her name is the root of the word "hygiene." Hygeia was typically portrayed as a young woman feeding a serpent from a bowl. This serpent is a symbol of medicine and renewal, and the bowl represents nourishment and well-being. Unlike her father, who was associated with curing illness, Hygeia focused on prevention and maintaining good health. Temples dedicated to Asclepius often included shrines to Hygeia, emphasizing the importance of both curative and preventative care. Her cult was widespread throughout the Greek world, and she was later adopted by the Romans under the name Salus. The concept of public health and personal hygiene are directly linked to her influence, making her a significant figure in the history of medicine and well-being. Her emphasis on preventative measures remains relevant in modern healthcare.
